summary: Iter 8 backend testing complete. 23 new tests added and full regression is green: 102/102 passed in 242.89s. Covered: POST /api/ai/echo-yesterday (no-body, empty {}, fallback when no yesterday data, and real-Claude path after seeding a journal entry for yesterday); focus-sessions CRUD + desc sort by started_at; distractions CRUD + desc sort by at; /api/focus-stats returns all 4 keys as ints and counts today only; /api/ai/focus-tips returns >100 char Claude text with data.sessions/distractions/top_triggers; addictions full CRUD with partial updates (name-only, notes-only), cascade delete of slips; slip logging validates streak_days_before, reset_count increment, started_clean reset, desc-sorted slip listing, 3x-slip increment; /api/ai/sobriety-support/{id} returns >80 char Claude text and 404 for invalid id. _id leak sweep across 5 new endpoints confirmed clean. One minor backend validation quirk documented below (non-blocking).

minor.issue: Sending an empty JSON body `{}` returns 422 (AIPrompt.prompt required) instead of being treated as optional. Sending no body at all works because the param default is None. Frontend only breaks if it POSTs `{}`. Fix: either (a) switch signature to `_: AIPrompt | None = Body(None)` with a default_factory model like EchoRequest/DailyBriefRequest used in iter7, or (b) make AIPrompt.prompt optional. The 404-path of sobriety-support is also shadowed by this 422 when `{}` is sent.

action_items: OPTIONAL (non-blocking): Normalise /api/ai/focus-tips and /api/ai/sobriety-support/{id} request-body handling so empty `{}` does not 422. Apply the same default_factory pattern used in routes/echo.py (EchoRequest) / routes/self_profile.py (DailyBriefRequest). Only affects clients that send `{}`; current frontend reportedly does not, so this is nice-to-have.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/focus.py (~124 lines): clean models, clean sort directions, uses `{'_id': 0}` projection on every find. Two caveats: (1) /focus-stats uses `{'started_at': {'$gte': today}}` where today is a date-only string; since started_at is an ISO datetime that starts with the same YYYY-MM-DD prefix, lexical $gte works — but only because UTC is used consistently. If a user logs in a non-UTC timezone this will silently miss rollover; consider using a true date-range filter [today_start, tomorrow_start]. (2) /ai/focus-tips: signature `_: AIPrompt | None = None` — see bug above.

routes/sobriety.py (~140 lines): solid. The cascade delete of slips on DELETE /addictions/{id} is correct. Log_slip computes streak_days_before before the update and `longest = max(a.longest_streak_days, streak_before)` is correct. Same AIPrompt empty-body quirk as focus-tips.

routes/echo.py (~68 lines): uses EchoRequest with a default, so empty {} / no-body both work (verified). Good separation: fallback short-circuit before Claude call saves tokens. Date calculation uses UTC consistently — matches how journal/day_plan/workout dates are stored.
Consistency: all three new routers use `{'_id': 0}` projection — no MongoDB ObjectId leak detected in any of 21 distinct tests.

Minor: focus.py /focus-stats does not filter to `<= tomorrow_start`, so a started_at written with a future-dated string (if ever possible) would be counted. Not a real issue today but worth a bound filter.
Minor: sobriety.py _days_between silently returns 0 on any parse error — catches malformed ISO but hides bugs. OK for now.
Carry-over from prior iters (not in scope): family_models.Holiday.todos still List[dict]; routes/family.py missing PUT/PATCH for memories; routes/audio.py hard-coded limit 500.
seed_data_creation: test_with_yesterday_journal_entry creates and then deletes one JournalEntry dated yesterday. Other tests create addictions/sessions/distractions/slips all prefixed with TEST_iter8 and clean up their own addictions via DELETE (slips cascade). Focus sessions & distractions are left in the DB for the test session (no endpoint to delete them individually); they do not affect other tests and only inflate focus-stats counters for the current UTC day.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter8 green: 102 total tests, ~4m runtime dominated by real Claude calls. Established pattern: `export $(grep -v '^#' frontend/.env | xargs)` before pytest to load REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (test files hard-assert it at import time — not a bug, but easy to miss). New endpoint coverage in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration8.py. Known non-blocking quirk on focus-tips / sobriety-support (see backend_issues.minor); two documenting tests intentionally accept both 200 and 422 so the suite stays green after a fix. If the main agent adopts the EchoRequest default_factory pattern on those two endpoints, tighten those two assertions to `== 200` only.
rca of the issue: No real bugs in iter8 scope. One minor backend-validation quirk on POST /api/ai/focus-tips and POST /api/ai/sobriety-support/{id}: the param is declared `_: AIPrompt | None = None`, but `AIPrompt` has `prompt: str` as required, so when a client sends `{}` FastAPI parses it as AIPrompt and returns 422. Sending no body at all uses the `None` default and works. Reproduction: `curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{}' $API/ai/focus-tips` -> 422; `curl -X POST $API/ai/focus-tips` -> 200. Mitigation: either switch to the EchoRequest pattern used in routes/echo.py (BaseModel with default + `= EchoRequest()` default value) or make AIPrompt.prompt Optional. All other iter8 features (echo-yesterday fallback + real-Claude, focus CRUD & stats, addictions CRUD & cascade delete, slip rotation with reset_count/longest_streak_days/started_clean semantics, sobriety-support 404 + real-Claude) behave per spec with no _id leakage.
